_comment: Committed connection config — never real credentials; the WordPress values are ${VAR:-} env placeholders (tracked file). The elementor connection authenticates when WP_URL / WP_USERNAME / WP_APP_PASSWORD are set — the same vars wordpress-api-pro uses, so one env set targets the same site with both toolkits. On devices set them in the shell; in claude.ai cloud environments set them in the environment's env vars (one environment per client site works well). The :- defaults keep the config parseable when the vars are unset — the connection then just shows as unavailable in /mcp until they're provided. The proxy version is pinned (bump deliberately; the config is auto-approved, so an unpinned latest would be a supply-chain risk). Per-site local setups keep using files/setup-elementor-mcp.sh, which writes a gitignored per-project config and refuses to overwrite this tracked one.
